title: LG Washing Machine OE Error Code
summary: Water won't drain from the tub
meta_description: LG washer OE means the drain cycle timed out. Clean the pump filter, check the drain hose, and clear suds blockages.
meaning: OE is LG's Outlet Error. The drain pump ran but water remained in the tub after the allowed drain window—about 5–7 minutes on most front-loaders, or up to 13 minutes on some top-load models. The washer stops before spin to avoid leaving a full tub.
causes: Pump filter clogged with lint, coins, or hair ties
Drain hose kinked behind the washer or pushed too far into the standpipe
Excess suds from non-HE detergent creating air pockets in the pump
Frozen drain hose or standpipe in cold weather installations
fixes: Open the bottom-front service panel, drain the emergency hose into a shallow pan, then unscrew and clean the pump filter
Straighten the drain hose and keep 4–10 inches clearance from the wall so it cannot kink
Disconnect the hose at the standpipe and confirm water flows freely during a Spin cycle test
Run a Tub Clean cycle with reduced detergent if suds were visible before the error
when_to_call_technician: Call service if the pump hums during spin but water never moves—the drain pump motor or impeller likely failed.
deep_dive: When OE appears on a LG washing machine, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG WM3400CW, LG WM3900HWA, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most OE calls are resolved without parts: open the bottom-front service panel, drain the emergency hose into a shallow pan, then unscrew and clean the pump filter. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If OE returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use washing machines to prevent OE from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when OE appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your LG washing machine—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for OE; LG uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting OE; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: LG Washing Machine LE Error Code
summary: Locked motor—rotor position sensor did not confirm drum rotation
meta_description: LG washer LE is locked motor, not Samsung leak LE. Test hall sensor 6501KW2002A (5–15 kΩ), rear panel rotor bolt on WM4000HWA and WM3900HWA.
meaning: LG washing machine LE indicates a Locked Motor fault—the inverter drive energizes the stator but the rotor position sensor (RPS/hall sensor) does not confirm expected drum movement within the detection window. This is unrelated to Samsung LE (leak). On WM4000HWA and WM3900HWA direct-drive platforms, LE commonly traces to a failed hall sensor 6501KW2002A ($12–35), foreign object jamming the drum, or a seized bearing—not the main PCB as a first guess. The sensor mounts on the stator at the rear; resistance typically reads 5–15 kΩ at room temperature depending on pin pair.
causes: Failed rotor position hall sensor 6501KW2002A reading open or out of 5–15 kΩ spec
Loose or corroded harness connector at the hall sensor or inverter board
Physical drum jam from bra wire, coin, or sock between tub and basket
Seized tub bearing causing rotor stall under load—often preceded by rumble noise
Overloaded drum stalling motor on heavy items before sensor confirms rotation
Inverter board fault not commutating stator windings—after sensor and mechanical rules out
Damaged rotor magnets or stator wiring from prior service misalignment
fixes: Unplug washer; attempt to rotate drum by hand through the door—note any grinding or hard spots indicating mechanical jam.
Remove rear panel screws; inspect harness from inverter to hall sensor for chafing or loose clip at stator.
Disconnect hall sensor harness; measure resistance on 6501KW2002A per LG chart—replace if outside 5–15 kΩ or open.
If mechanical bind exists, remove rotor bolt (secure rotor with strap—it is magnetic), pull rotor, inspect for foreign object between stator and rotor air gap.
Install new 6501KW2002A fully seated in stator pocket; route harness away from rotor bolt path; reinstall rotor and torque bolt to spec.
Reconnect rear panel; plug in; run Spin Only with empty drum—confirm LE cleared before loading.
If LE persists with new sensor and free drum rotation, test inverter board connections or schedule professional stator/inverter diagnosis.
when_to_call_technician: Request LG-trained service when LE continues after hall sensor replacement and confirmed free drum rotation, or when bearing rumble accompanies LE—stator/inverter replacement exceeds typical DIY scope. WM3900HWA stacked units need secure bracing before rear service. Have sensor part 6501KW2002A or exact sub revision from the removed unit when ordering.
deep_dive: LG direct-drive washers eliminate belts by mounting the rotor directly on the drum shaft. The hall sensor 6501KW2002A reads magnetic pole position so the inverter can commutate the stator windings. When the sensor reads open, short, or erratic—or when the drum physically cannot turn—LE latches before or during wash agitation and spin. Overloading with wet heavy comforters can stall the drum momentarily and trigger intermittent LE that clears after power cycle; true sensor failure repeats on light loads.

Rear panel access is required for meaningful LE diagnosis. Unplug the washer, remove the rear cover screws, and locate the large circular rotor secured by a single central bolt. Do not remove the rotor until you photograph wire routing—the hall sensor harness routes along the stator and is easy to pinch on reassembly. With the belt-less design, rotate the drum by hand from inside the tub: smooth rotation suggests electrical fault; grinding or hard stop suggests bearing, coin under tub, or motor jam.

Multimeter testing of 6501KW2002A uses the service chart pin pairs—typically 5–15 kΩ. Open or zero ohms confirms replacement without removing the stator. When replacing, align the sensor fully into the stator pocket until the clip seats; an air gap causes intermittent LE only at high RPM. Some WM4000HWA revisions use updated sensor subtypes—match the part number on the old sensor body.

Related LG codes OE (dishwasher drain—not this appliance), UE (unbalance), and dE (door lock) often appear in owner searches alongside LE. Document that your LE is on an LG washer motor circuit before following Samsung or Bosch LE guides—cross-brand advice causes misdiagnosis. If LE followed a power surge, inspect the noise filter board at the rear for burnt traces before ordering a sensor.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing heavy comforters alone on direct-drive LG units—LE often starts as momentary motor stall under overload.
Keep rear panel harness clips engaged after any service—pinched hall sensor wires cause intermittent LE.
Replace hall sensor at first confirmed ohm failure rather than repeatedly power-cycling, which stresses the inverter.
part_cost_estimate: Hall sensor 6501KW2002A: $12–35; inverter board if needed: $150–280

title: LG Washing Machine UE Error Code
meta_description: LG UE error stops spin when laundry is unevenly distributed. Redistribute clothes, level the washer, avoid single heavy items alone.
meaning: UE is an Unbalanced Error. Before reaching full spin speed, LG washers measure drum movement; if weight is piled on one side, the machine pauses and displays UE rather than shake violently. Single heavy items like bath mats are a frequent trigger.
causes: One heavy garment clumped against the drum wall
Washer not level—front legs uneven or nut not locked
Worn shock absorbers allowing excessive tub movement
Small load with insufficient items to balance the drum
fixes: Pause the cycle, open the door, and spread clothes around the entire drum perimeter
Add one or two towels when washing a single heavy item alone
Place a level on the washer top and adjust front legs until the bubble centers, then tighten lock nuts
Retry on Spin Only at 800 RPM before attempting a full wash cycle
when_to_call_technician: If UE appears on balanced full loads after leveling, worn suspension rods or shock absorbers inside the tub assembly likely need replacement.
deep_dive: When UE appears on a LG washing machine, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG WM8100HVA, LG WM4000HWA, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most UE calls are resolved without parts: pause the cycle, open the door, and spread clothes around the entire drum perimeter. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If UE returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use washing machines to prevent UE from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when UE appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your LG washing machine—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for UE; LG uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting UE; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: LG Washing Machine dE Error Code
summary: Door lock fault—control did not receive locked confirmation before fill
meta_description: LG washer dE door lock before fill: check gasket clothing, strike alignment on WM3400CW and WM4000HWA; not dishwasher dE.
meaning: LG washing machine dE (also shown as dE1 or dE2 on some displays) means the door lock circuit did not confirm a secure lock before the cycle proceeded to fill or spin. The main PCB energizes the door lock motor; a switch in the latch assembly must report locked status. If clothing jams the gasket, if the strike is misaligned, or if the lock motor or switch fails, dE appears within seconds of start. This is the LG washer door code—not LG dishwasher display variants. WM3400CW and WM4000HWA front loaders share the same latch family; related OE on LG refers to dishwasher drain, not washer dE.
causes: Clothing or towel edge trapped in door gasket preventing full lock engagement
Misaligned door strike on cabinet hook after cabinet tilt or loose front feet
Worn door latch assembly or failed lock motor not completing travel
Lock switch inside latch not reporting closed despite audible motor clicks
Damaged door boot warp or tear at latch zone changing door geometry
Wire harness fracture in door boot flex zone from repeated door opens
Control Lock or Child Lock enabled—displays similar to latch faults on some firmware
fixes: Cancel cycle; open door; pull gasket lip and remove any fabric caught in the seal channel especially at bottom center.
Close door firmly with one hand on the upper glass—listen for single solid lock click before pressing Start.
Place level on washer top; adjust front feet until cabinet is square; verify strike aligns centered in latch pocket.
Apply thin film of silicone grease to strike contact point if plastic wear marks are visible.
Disable Child Lock per owner manual (usually hold Temp + Options three seconds) if lock icon is lit.
If dE persists, unplug washer; remove top console screws; inspect door lock harness at boot for broken wires.
Replace door lock assembly if motor clicks three times without cycle start—match part to WM3400CW serial chart.
when_to_call_technician: Call service when dE continues after gasket clearing, leveling, and strike lubrication, with confirmed Child Lock off. Lock motor replacement or main PCB door circuit testing requires LG tools. WM4000HWA integrated in laundry closets may need two-person lift for console access—provide exact model/sub for latch part.
deep_dive: LG front-load door locks use a motor-driven latch with dual reporting switches for redundancy. dE on WM3400CW often traces to fabric caught between door glass and gasket at the six-o'clock position— the door looks closed but micro-gap prevents lock motor from completing travel. Pull the gasket lip outward slightly and reload before assuming part failure.

Strike alignment on the cabinet hook relative to the latch pocket affects every cycle after heavy use. If the washer cabinet sagged because front feet are uneven, the strike misses the latch by millimeters. Level the washer and visually center the strike in the latch opening before adjusting parts. LG service bulletins recommend silicone grease on the strike wear point when plastic shows scoring.

Listen for lock motor actuation when dE appears: three rapid clicks usually mean the motor ran but the switch did not confirm. Silence may mean harness disconnect at the door boot or failed lock assembly. Access wiring through the front top console after unplugging—continuity tests on the lock switches require the service manual chart for your WM4000HWA revision.

Child Lock or Control Lock enabled can mimic dE on some LG UI flows—verify the lock icon is not lit on the panel. dE differs from LE (motor) and UE (balance): dE always occurs at cycle start before drum motion, never mid-spin. After replacing latch, run a rinse cycle empty to confirm lock engages before loading full wash.
prevention_tips: Load laundry without pressing fabric against the door gasket closure line—dE most often starts with a trapped sock hem.
Level LG front loaders after every move; strike misalignment from tilt causes chronic dE without part failure.
Avoid slamming the door; strike and latch wear accelerates when the cabinet flexes on impact.
part_cost_estimate: Door lock assembly: $45–95; door boot if torn: $80–140

title: LG Washing Machine FE Error Code
meta_description: LG FE error indicates too much water in the tub. Shut off supply valves immediately and inspect the inlet valve and pressure hose.
meaning: FE is a Fill Error tied to overfill detection. The pressure switch or water level sensor reports more water than expected—sometimes because an inlet valve stuck open, the pressure hose disconnected, or the drain failed and water accumulated between cycles.
causes: Inlet valve stuck open due to mineral buildup on the solenoid
Pressure switch air dome tube disconnected from the tub
Drain failure leaving water from a prior cycle
Control board relay welded closed on the water valve circuit
fixes: Turn off hot and cold supply valves behind the washer immediately
Unplug the machine and inspect the clear pressure hose from the tub dome to the control panel
Reconnect or replace the hose if cracked, kinked, or loose at either end
With water off, plug back in and run a Drain cycle only—do not run Fill until FE clears
when_to_call_technician: If the tub fills while the washer is unplugged, both inlet valves are stuck open and must be replaced before use.
deep_dive: When FE appears on a LG washing machine, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG WM9500HKA, LG WM8100HVA, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most FE calls are resolved without parts: turn off hot and cold supply valves behind the washer immediately. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If FE returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use washing machines to prevent FE from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when FE appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your LG washing machine—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for FE; LG uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting FE; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: LG Washing Machine PE Error Code
meta_description: LG PE error means the pressure sensor cannot read tub water level. Check the air dome hose and pressure switch connection.
meaning: PE indicates a Pressure Error—the water level sensor is not sending a valid signal to the main board. The washer may refuse to fill, drain, or advance cycles because it cannot determine how much water is in the tub.
causes: Air dome hose disconnected or cracked between tub and pressure switch
Pressure switch diaphragm failed or clogged with detergent residue
Loose wire connector at the pressure sensor on the control board
Main control board pressure input circuit damaged
fixes: Unplug the washer and locate the thin clear hose running from the tub to the top control housing
Remove the hose at both ends and blow through it—air should pass freely with no blockage
Reattach the hose firmly to the tub air dome nipple and pressure switch port
Run a small load cycle and watch whether fill stops at the correct level
when_to_call_technician: If the hose is intact and PE returns, the pressure switch or control board input needs bench testing with a multimeter.
deep_dive: When PE appears on a LG washing machine, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG WM3400CW, LG WM3700HWA, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most PE calls are resolved without parts: unplug the washer and locate the thin clear hose running from the tub to the top control housing. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If PE returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.

title: LG Washing Machine tE Error Code
meta_description: LG tE error means the water temperature sensor signal is invalid. Inspect the thermistor wiring and heater circuit.
meaning: tE is a Thermistor Error. The NTC sensor that reports wash water temperature sent a resistance value outside the expected range—open circuit, short, or a reading that never changes during heated cycles.
causes: Thermistor connector loose at the tub or heater assembly
Damaged sensor wire rubbing against the tub during spin
Failed NTC thermistor element
Heater circuit fault causing erratic temperature feedback
fixes: Unplug the washer and inspect the two-wire connector on the tub thermistor
Reseat the connector and check wires for burn marks near the heater terminals
Run a warm wash and carefully feel door-glass temperature mid-cycle
Power-cycle and retry—occasional tE can clear after a single board reset
when_to_call_technician: Technicians compare thermistor ohms against the LG service chart at room temperature; replacement is needed if readings are open.
deep_dive: LG washing machine models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. TE means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On LG WM3900HWA, LG WM4000HWA, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Thermistor connector loose at the tub or heater assembly is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, unplug the washer and inspect the two-wire connector on the tub thermistor. If the latch clicks but TE persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most LG units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—TE often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when TE appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your LG washing machine—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for TE; LG uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting TE; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: LG Washing Machine IE Error Code
meta_description: LG IE error means the washer did not detect incoming water. Open supply valves and clean inlet hose mesh screens.
meaning: IE is an Inlet Error. The inlet valves opened but the water level sensor never registered a rise within the fill timeout—so the board assumes no water entered the tub.
causes: Hot or cold supply valve closed or partially shut
Clogged mesh screens inside the inlet hose connections at the washer
Kinked inlet hoses pinned against the wall
Failed inlet valve solenoid on one side of a dual valve
fixes: Turn both wall supply valves fully counterclockwise
Unscrew inlet hoses from the back of the washer and rinse the mesh filters in a bucket
Straighten hoses and pull the machine forward slightly to remove kinks
Run a cold-fill cycle and watch through the door glass for incoming water
when_to_call_technician: If water flows strongly at the hose ends but the tub stays empty, the inlet valve or water level sensor requires professional diagnosis.

title: LG Washing Machine HE Error Code
meta_description: LG HE washer error indicates the heating element did not raise water temperature. Check heater connections and incoming hot water supply.
meaning: HE on select LG washers with internal heaters means the element did not bring wash water to the target temperature within the programmed time. Models without a heater may show HE for a related heating or thermistor fault—check your manual.
causes: Household water heater below 120°F delivering lukewarm fill water
Open heating element or burned terminal at the tub heater
Tripped thermal fuse on the heater assembly
Thermistor giving false cold readings to the board
fixes: Run the kitchen tap until hot before starting a warm or sanitize cycle
Verify the water heater thermostat is set to at least 120°F
Unplug and inspect heater terminals under the tub for corrosion or burn marks
Retry on a warm cycle after confirming hot water at the wall valves
when_to_call_technician: Element and thermal fuse testing requires accessing the tub heater from the rear or bottom—schedule service if water stays cold through a warm cycle.

title: LG Washing Machine SE Error Code

Damaged stator wiring from vibration

title: LG Washing Machine AE Error Code
meta_description: LG AE on some washers indicates a leak sensor or auto-shutoff event. Inspect hoses, door boot, and tub seals for moisture.
meaning: AE indicates an Auto Error shutoff related to leak or overflow protection on certain LG models. The machine stopped incoming water and locked out further operation until the leak path is identified and dried.
causes: Loose drain hose clamp leaking during spin
Torn door boot allowing water to escape at the front
Over-sudding pushing foam through the door seal

Examine the gray door boot fold for pinholes or tears at the bottom

Dry any moisture at the base and run a short rinse to confirm no new leaks

title: LG Washing Machine CE Error Code
meta_description: LG CE error means the main and display boards lost communication. Try a power reset; persistent CE needs board diagnosis.
meaning: CE is a Communication Error between the main control board and the sub-PCB or display module. Cycles may freeze mid-program or the panel may flash CE without responding to buttons.
causes: Loose ribbon cable between display and main board
Power surge damaging a board communication chip
Moisture or condensation on board connectors
Failed sub-PCB on models with separate inverter communication
fixes: Unplug the washer for five full minutes, then restore power

Avoid using the machine on an unprotected outlet during storms
If CE appears at startup every time, photograph the code and model plate for service
when_to_call_technician: CE after power reset usually requires main board or display PCB replacement with model-matched parts.
deep_dive: When CE appears on a LG washing machine, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG WM3900HWA, LG WM3700HWA, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most CE calls are resolved without parts: unplug the washer for five full minutes, then restore power. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If CE returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.

Pour warm (not boiling) water over accessible hose sections until flow returns
After thawing, run a warm fill cycle and confirm FF clears before regular use

title: LG Washing Machine PF Error Code
meta_description: LG PF means power failed during a cycle. Press Start to resume or drain if water remains in the tub.
meaning: PF is a Power Failure code—not a component fault. The control board lost AC power during a cycle from a tripped breaker, brief outage, or someone unplugging the unit. LG stores enough state to offer resume on some models.
causes: Tripped circuit breaker or blown fuse on the laundry circuit
Loose power cord at the outlet or terminal block
Brief neighborhood power outage during the cycle
Using an extension cord that cannot sustain motor start current
fixes: Check the breaker panel and reset any tripped laundry-room circuit
Press Start—many LG models offer Resume if power returned within a few minutes
If water sits in the tub, run Drain + Spin before restarting the full cycle
Plug directly into a grounded 120V outlet rated for 15A—avoid extension cords
when_to_call_technician: If PF appears while other appliances on the same circuit work fine, inspect the washer power cord and terminal block for heat damage.
deep_dive: LG washing machine models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. PF means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On LG WM3400CW, LG WM3900HWA, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Tripped circuit breaker or blown fuse on the laundry circuit is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, check the breaker panel and reset any tripped laundry-room circuit. If the latch clicks but PF persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most LG units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—PF often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

title: LG Dryer D80 Error Code
summary: Flow Sense detected roughly 80% vent restriction blocking exhaust airflow
meta_description: LG D80 means ~80% vent blockage on Flow Sense dryers. Clean the lint filter, flex duct, and exterior hood on DLE7300WE and DLEX4000W models—not a gas supply fault.
meaning: LG D80 is a vent-restriction warning on dryers equipped with Flow Sense monitoring. The control compares exhaust temperature rise and airflow characteristics against expected profiles; when back-pressure indicates roughly 80% blockage, D80 displays before the dryer enters a hard shutdown. On electric models such as DLE7300WE and DLEX4000W, D80 almost always means lint-limited airflow through the duct path—not a heating element failure. Note: on some LG gas dryers, a displayed d80 can relate to gas supply issues; on electric Flow Sense units, treat D80 as a vent hygiene fault first. Uncorrected restriction progresses to D90 and D95 with longer dry times and higher element stress.
causes: Lint filter loaded or embedded with fine mesh clog despite appearing clean on quick inspection
Crushed, kinked, or excessively long flexible duct behind the dryer restricting 4-inch exhaust path
Exterior vent hood damper stuck closed from paint, lint glue, or insect nesting
Duct run exceeding recommended equivalent length (multiple elbows without smooth-wall rigid pipe)
Internal blower wheel coated with lint at the drum outlet reducing fan efficiency before the duct even starts
Incorrect duct diameter (3-inch reducers on 4-inch LG exhaust port)
On gas LG units only: misread d80 related to gas supply—verify gas valve open before assuming vent fault on DLG7301WE
fixes: Remove the lint filter; wash both screens in warm water with mild detergent, dry completely, and reinstall before any test cycle.
Unplug the dryer, pull it forward, loosen the clamp on the rear exhaust port, and vacuum the blower housing opening visible through the duct hole.
Replace crushed white vinyl flex with UL-listed semi-rigid or rigid aluminum duct; keep total run under manufacturer equivalent length and minimize 90° elbows.
From outside, open the hood damper manually, remove lint packed behind the flap, and confirm it closes but does not bind when released.

Disconnect duct from dryer rear and run Air Dry or Timed Dry 10 minutes venting into the room (with window ventilation); if D80 does not return, reassemble duct section by section to find the choke point.
Use a vent brush or professional rotary cleaning kit through the full duct run from exterior toward the dryer.
Inspect blower wheel through service panel if D80 persists with open duct: rotate wheel by hand—it should spin freely without rubbing the housing.
when_to_call_technician: Schedule service if D80 returns immediately after full duct replacement and filter cleaning, with the duct disconnected at the rear for a test cycle. That suggests a failed Flow Sense thermistor, wiring to the exhaust sensor, or a blower wheel separated from the motor shaft. On DLG7301WE gas dryers, if clothes stay cold and you smell no gas ignition, mention gas supply to the tech—gas models can display d80 for non-vent causes.
deep_dive: Flow Sense on LG DLE7300WE, DLEX4000W, and DLG7301WE (gas hybrid reporting) uses sensors in the exhaust path to infer how freely moist air leaves the drum. When flex duct is crushed behind the dryer, when the exterior hood flap sticks shut, or when lint packs the filter housing channels, the exhaust profile matches LG's ~80% blocked threshold and D80 appears—often mid-cycle with clothes still damp and the drum warm but not hot enough for efficient evaporation.

D80 is intentionally early warning. LG's escalation path runs D80 → D90 (~90% blocked) → D95 (severe restriction or sensor fault). Homeowners who ignore D80 often see TE (thermistor) or HS (humidity sensor) companion codes later because the control compensates by extending heat until safety limits trip. Cleaning at D80 prevents element and fuse damage that makes a simple duct cleaning into a $150+ parts call.

Start every D80 diagnosis at the lint filter—not just wiping the screen but washing the filter mesh in warm soapy water monthly on LG's dual-screen designs. Lint embedded in the screen weave restricts air even when the surface looks clean. Next, pull the dryer 18–24 inches from the wall, disconnect the 4-inch flex duct from the rear exhaust port, and inspect for accordion crushing; rigid or semi-rigid aluminum duct per local code reduces D80 recurrence compared to white vinyl flex.

Exterior termination matters: bird nests, stuck dampers, and builder-grade hoods with tiny openings are the most common 'cleaned everything inside' failures. From outside, verify the damper swings freely when you push it with a gloved hand. Run a timed dry with the duct disconnected to the room (only for a short test with adequate ventilation); if D80 clears, the restriction is entirely in the duct run or hood—not the dryer internals.
prevention_tips: Wash LG lint filters monthly and never run without the filter seated—bypassing the screen sends lint directly into the blower and duct.
Use rigid or semi-rigid 4-inch duct with short runs; inspect the exterior hood each season for stuck dampers.
Stop and clean vents at first D80 before LG escalates to D90/D95 and forces extended high-heat cycles that stress the element.
part_cost_estimate: Rigid duct and hood upgrade: $25–80; blower wheel if damaged: $40–90

title: LG Dryer AE Error Code
meta_description: LG AE dryer code triggers automatic shutdown. Inspect for overheating, vent blockage, or control faults after AE appears.
meaning: AE is an Auto Error shutoff—the control board stopped operation to protect components, often following overheating, repeated sensor faults, or an internal self-test failure.
causes: Severe vent restriction causing repeated high-limit trips
Moisture sensor short from fabric softener sheet residue
Main board detecting abnormal current draw
Condenser or heat-pump coil iced on ventless models
fixes: Clear the full vent path if any D80/D90 code appeared recently
Clean moisture sensor bars inside the drum with rubbing alcohol
Unplug for ten minutes to reset protective logic
On heat-pump models, ensure room temperature is within the manual's operating range
when_to_call_technician: AE that returns on the first cycle after vent cleaning needs board or heat-pump system diagnosis.
deep_dive: Heating faults such as AE appear when the LG dryer control energizes the bake element, broil element, gas valve, or drying heater but does not detect expected temperature rise within the monitoring window. On LG DLEX9000V, LG DLHC5502W, and similar variants, open heating elements, failed igniters on gas dryers and ranges, or welded heater relays on the control board produce similar symptoms.

Severe vent restriction causing repeated high-limit trips should be ruled out first. clear the full vent path if any d80/d90 code appeared recently. On electric ovens and dryers, test element continuity when cool; on gas appliances verify the ignitor glows for at least 15 seconds before the valve opens. Repeated AE after confirming heat at the exhaust or oven cavity indicates a sensor or board issue rather than a simple element replacement.
prevention_tips: Preheat the oven only as directed and avoid foil lining the bottom—blocked airflow causes heat faults related to AE.

title: LG Dryer dE Error Code

causes: Door not fully shut against the gasket

Door switch lever stuck from lint at the hinge

title: LG Dryer CL Error Code
summary: Child lock is active—not a fault
meta_description: LG CL means Control Lock is on. Hold the child lock button 3 seconds to disable—dryer is not broken.
meaning: CL displays Child Lock engaged. The control panel buttons are disabled to prevent accidental cycle changes—this is intentional, not a malfunction.
causes: Child Lock enabled by holding two buttons per manual
SmartThinQ app lock turned on remotely
Control lock after cleaning the panel while pressing buttons
Factory default lock setting on display demo units mistakenly left enabled
fixes: Press and hold the Child Lock or Rack Dry button for three seconds until CL clears
Check the SmartThinQ app for an active control lock toggle
Refer to the button icon chart on the panel bezel for your model's exact combo
Unplug two minutes only if the lock combo is unknown—note this resets some settings
when_to_call_technician: No service needed for CL alone—only if buttons remain dead after lock is cleared.
deep_dive: When CL appears on a LG dryer, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG DLEX3900W, LG DLEX4000W, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most CL calls are resolved without parts: press and hold the child lock or rack dry button for three seconds until cl clears. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If CL returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use dryers to prevent CL from lint and food debris buildup.

title: LG Dishwasher OE Error Code
summary: Outlet error—drain pump ran but water did not leave within timeout
meta_description: LG dishwasher OE is drain timeout—not washer OE. Clean tub floor filter, high loop drain hose 30cm+, force drain Delicate+Dual Control; pump 5859DD9001B.
meaning: LG dishwasher OE means Outlet Error: the drain pump energized for the programmed duration but the control did not detect sufficient water removal from the sump. This is specific to LG dishwashers—do not confuse it with LG washing machine OE (overflow) or Samsung OE codes. On LDFN4542S, LDTS5552S, and LDP6810SS models, OE typically reflects a restricted filter, blocked drain hose, failed drain pump impeller, or improper drain hose routing without the required high loop. The pump part 5859DD9001B is the common OEM drain motor replacement when mechanical clearing fails.
causes: Tub floor filter and sump area clogged with food debris restricting inlet to drain pump
Drain hose lacking 30 cm minimum high loop, allowing backflow from sink or disposal
Kinked drain hose behind dishwasher or under sink after recent cabinet adjustment
Garbage disposal clog or full knockout obstruction at the shared drain port
Drain pump impeller cracked or jammed by seeds, broken glass, or toothpick fragments
Failed drain pump motor 5859DD9001B—hums without moving water or silent with good harness voltage
Air gap (if installed) clogged at the vent cap, blocking atmospheric break in drain path
fixes: Unplug the dishwasher, remove lower rack, and lift the flat filter cover; pull the cylindrical filter and rinse all mesh layers under hot water.
Reach into the sump cavity with a flashlight; remove visible debris; rotate the impeller gently clockwise to confirm it moves without grinding.
Trace the drain hose from the pump outlet to the sink; create or verify a high loop peaking at least 30 cm above the floor connection; secure with a cabinet screw tab.
Disconnect the hose at the disposal or tailpiece; drain residual water into a pan; flush the hose with water from a funnel to dislodge grease plugs.
Run LG force drain: hold Delicate + Dual Control three seconds (confirm combo on your LDP6810SS label); listen for pump operation and water at sink.
If OE persists, remove toe kick, lay towels, access pump from underside, disconnect harness, twist pump body counterclockwise to unlock, and inspect impeller for damage.
Install replacement pump 5859DD9001B with sump clear, bayonet aligned, twist clockwise until locked, reconnect harness, and run rinse-only test before loading dishes.
when_to_call_technician: Call service if force drain fails silently (no pump hum) or hums without water movement after filter and hose are verified clear. Harness or main PCB drain relay faults require live voltage testing. Provide model LDFN4542S or LDTS5552S from the door rating plate when ordering the 5859DD9001B pump to match revision.
deep_dive: LG dishwashers monitor drain progress through pressure or timing algorithms depending on series. When the sump still holds water after the pump runs—often 90 seconds to two minutes—OE latches on the display. Unlike Bosch E24 wording, LG OE explicitly flags outlet path failure. Start at the tub-floor filter assembly: LG uses a flat filter cover and cylindrical fine filter; debris under the cover prevents the impeller from drawing water even when the pump motor spins.

Drain hose routing is critical on LG LDFN4542S and similar: the hose must rise at least 30 cm (roughly 12 inches) from the floor connection before descending to the disposal or tailpiece. A missing high loop allows back-siphon from the sink or disposal, refilling the sump after the pump stops and causing OE on the next drain attempt. Zip-tie the hose to the cabinet top or air-gap fitting, not low on the rear panel.

LG provides a force-drain procedure on many models: press and hold Delicate plus Dual Control (or the combination listed on your model sticker) for three seconds to activate manual pump out without a full cycle. Use this after clearing the filter to verify pump output into the sink drain before re-running a wash. If force drain works but OE returns mid-cycle, suspect intermittent hose blockage or a weak pump that cannot sustain flow under wash-arm back-pressure.

When replacing the drain pump 5859DD9001B ($60–120), access is from beneath after removing the toe panel and tipping the unit slightly backward with protection on the floor. Mark wire harness orientation, twist-lock the pump bayonet counterclockwise to remove, and clear the sump cavity of glass before installing the new pump—LG impellers chip easily if debris remains.
prevention_tips: Clean the LG tub floor filter weekly in heavy-use households—OE rarely appears when the sump inlet stays clear.
Maintain the 30 cm drain hose high loop after any dishwasher pull-forward for floor cleaning.
Run disposal before starting the dishwasher when sharing a drain port to prevent back-pressure OE faults.

title: LG Dishwasher CE Error Code
meta_description: LG CE on dishwashers often pairs with motor issues. Power-cycle; if CE persists with LE, motor service is likely.
meaning: CE indicates a circulation or communication fault on the wash motor circuit—distinct from LE in some firmware versions but often appearing together when the motor cannot report speed to the board.
causes: Motor harness unplugged during filter service
Control board motor driver failure
Sump debris intermittently jamming the impeller
Power surge damaging motor inverter circuitry
fixes: Shut off power 15 minutes and retry on a rinse-only cycle
Confirm the filter is seated—misalignment can splash water onto connectors
Listen for a single click then silence at cycle start
Note whether CE alternates with LE for the technician
when_to_call_technician: CE after clean sump and reset usually means motor or main board replacement.
deep_dive: When CE appears on a LG dishwasher, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG LDF5545ST, LG LDP7808BD, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most CE calls are resolved without parts: shut off power 15 minutes and retry on a rinse-only cycle. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If CE returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use dishwashers to prevent CE from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when CE appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your LG dishwasher—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for CE; LG uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting CE; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: LG Dishwasher bE Error Code

meaning: bE is a Button Error—the control thinks a key is continuously pressed. Cycles won't start or may flash bE until the panel state clears.
causes: Moisture on touch panel after steam from opening mid-cycle
Physical debris under a membrane button
Child pressing buttons during dry cycle
Failed touch panel flex circuit

deep_dive: BE on LG dishwasher units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. LG LDP7808BD, LG LDP6797ST, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Moisture on touch panel after steam from opening mid-cycle is a leading trigger. Start with dry the control panel with a soft cloth and leave the door open 10 minutes. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated BE on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes BE.

title: LG Refrigerator rS Error Code
meta_description: LG rS means fresh food thermistor error. Reseat sensor at rear wall; replace if display temp is wrong.
meaning: rS is a Refrigerator Sensor error—the fresh food compartment thermistor failed to report valid temperature, causing erratic compressor cycling or warm fridge sections.
causes: Sensor slipped out of the air duct clip
Damaged wire in the fresh food liner
Corrosion at the main board connector
Rare main board analog input failure
fixes: Verify vents in the fridge are not blocked by tall containers
Power off and reseat the refrigerator thermistor connector
Compare actual food temp with display reading using a thermometer
Leave doors closed one hour after reseat before evaluating
when_to_call_technician: Open-circuit thermistor requires replacement—part number varies by liner style.
deep_dive: RS on LG refrigerator units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. LG LFXC22526S, LG LFXS26973S, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Sensor slipped out of the air duct clip is a leading trigger. Start with verify vents in the fridge are not blocked by tall containers. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated RS on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes RS.

title: LG Oven F4 Error Code
meta_description: LG F4 means open oven sensor circuit. Check connector CN31 pins 4–5 for ~1.09kΩ at 25°C.
meaning: F4 is the open-circuit counterpart to F3—the temperature sensor or its wiring reads infinite resistance, so the control cannot detect oven temperature.
causes: Sensor connector CN31 loose at main PCB
Broken sensor wire inside the oven cavity insulation
Failed RTD element internally open
Previous repair left sensor unplugged
fixes: Power off and reseat CN31 at the main board
Measure sensor at the probe: open reading confirms replacement needed
Compare with LG spec chart printed on service manual for your model year
Retry preheat after reseat if ohms were borderline
when_to_call_technician: Open sensor with intact wiring requires probe replacement—access from inside the oven rear wall.
deep_dive: LG oven models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F4 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On LG LRG4115ST, LG LREL6325F, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Sensor connector CN31 loose at main PCB is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, power off and reseat cn31 at the main board. If the latch clicks but F4 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most LG units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F4 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

title: LG Oven F5 Error Code
meta_description: LG F5 relates to door lock motor or switch during clean or bake. Confirm door fully closed; service lock assembly if F5 persists.
meaning: F5 reports a door Lock circuit fault—the lock motor did not reach the expected position during self-clean or the lock switch feedback failed during normal bake on some models.
causes: Food soil preventing lock hook engagement
Failed door lock motor actuator
Broken lock switch
Warped door hinge misaligning the latch
fixes: Allow the oven to cool completely before opening if locked
Clean latch receiver on the frame after cool-down
Power-cycle after cooling—never force the door during F5
Run a standard Bake test before retrying self-clean
when_to_call_technician: Door lock motor replacement is required if F5 appears every self-clean attempt with a clean latch area.
deep_dive: When F5 appears on a LG oven, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On LG LREL6325F, LG LRG4113ST, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most F5 calls are resolved without parts: allow the oven to cool completely before opening if locked. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If F5 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use ovens to prevent F5 from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when F5 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your LG oven—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F21 Error Code
summary: Long drain—water did not leave the tub within the programmed timeout
meta_description: Whirlpool F21 drain timeout on WFW5605MW: access filter behind dispenser drawer, emergency hose, CCW filter cap; pump W10876600.
meaning: Whirlpool F21 indicates the drain system did not empty the tub within the allowed time—typically several minutes on front-load platforms. The control energizes the drain pump and monitors water level via the pressure switch; if level does not fall, F21 displays. On WFW5605MW, WFW6620HC, and WFW9150JW front loaders, the coin trap and filter hide behind the lower front dispenser drawer assembly—not behind a separate kick panel. F21 is the front-load counterpart to top-load F9 E1 on VMW/Cabrio families but uses the accessible front filter service path unique to Whirlpool Duet-style cabinets.
causes: Coin trap behind dispenser drawer packed with lint, coins, or debris blocking pump inlet
Filter cap not fully seated after cleaning, allowing air ingestion and poor pump prime
Kinked or crushed drain hose in stacked laundry closet between washer and standpipe
Drain pump W10876600 motor failed open or impeller separated from shaft
Excessive suds from non-HE detergent clogging trap mesh—SD may appear before F21
Foreign object in pump volute (wire, bra underwire) jamming impeller rotation
Pressure switch or hose fault reporting full tub when empty—rarer after verified physical drain
fixes: Unplug the washer; pull the dispenser drawer, press tab releases, remove drawer from rails to expose filter access.
Place a shallow pan under the emergency drain hose; remove the hose cap and drain residual water into the pan.
Turn the filter cap counterclockwise (CCW) until it releases; pull trap assembly; remove all debris; rinse under faucet.
Shine a flashlight into the pump opening; rotate impeller with a finger—it should spin freely without grinding.
Reinstall trap, thread cap clockwise hand-tight plus quarter turn; reattach emergency hose cap firmly.
Reinstall dispenser drawer on rails; plug in; run Drain/Spin and verify water discharges at standpipe within two minutes.
If F21 returns, tip washer back safely, remove rear bottom panel screws, locate pump W10876600, disconnect harness, and ohm motor per tech sheet.
when_to_call_technician: Schedule repair when the coin trap is clean, impeller spins freely, drain hose is clear, and F21 returns with the pump humming but no water movement—or when the pump is silent with verified 120 V at the harness during Drain/Spin. Pump replacement W10876600 requires tilting the washer and isolating water remaining in the sump. Mention WFW6620HC model for correct pump revision.
deep_dive: WFW5605MW F21 diagnosis starts at the dispenser drawer: pull the drawer fully out, press the two tab releases on the drawer slides, and lift the drawer off the rails. Behind the drawer cavity is the filter access door or exposed filter cap depending on revision. Whirlpool routes an emergency drain hose alongside the filter—have a shallow pan ready before opening the cap counterclockwise (CCW). Expect 1–3 quarts of residual water even when the tub looks empty.

The filter cap threads CCW for removal on most WFW units. Inside is a coin trap catching bobby pins, coins, and sock lint that bypass the door gasket. A packed trap lets the pump hum without moving water—the classic F21 with good hose routing. Clean the trap, inspect the pump impeller visible through the opening with a flashlight, and reinstall the cap hand-tight plus a quarter turn clockwise—cross-threading causes slow seep leaks that lead to F08 or floor moisture over weeks.

Drain hose checks mirror other brands: standpipe height, no kink behind the stacked dryer, and no excess insert depth causing siphon. Whirlpool front loads also fault F21 when the drain pump W10876600 fails electrically—after mechanical clearing, run Drain/Spin and listen: steady hum plus water at the standpipe means pump and path are good; humming dry or silence with F21 means pump motor or harness at the rear bottom panel.

SD (suds) can precede F21: oversudsing foam blocks the coin trap mesh and mimics pump failure. If F21 followed a high-suds cycle, run a Clean Washer cycle with affresh or no detergent before replacing the pump. Related codes F9 E1 (top load drain) and F8 E1 (long fill) share VMW platform wiring—document whether recent work disturbed the rear harness bundle when multiple codes appear in sequence.
prevention_tips: Empty pockets and check for small items before wash—coin trap cleaning every 3 months prevents most WFW5605MW F21 calls.
Use HE detergent only at marked dose lines to avoid SD suds that clog the trap and trigger F21.
After every filter service, confirm the cap is CCW-removed but clockwise re-tightened to seated position—loose caps cause repeat faults.
part_cost_estimate: Drain pump W10876600: $45–95; coin trap OEM: $20–35

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F08 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool washer F08 is a heater fault. Check element wiring, test heater resistance, verify incoming hot water supply.
meaning: F08 means the washer could not raise water temperature as expected—on Duet models the tub must pass 86°F within the first 40 minutes of a heated cycle. The heater circuit or element is not delivering heat the control board can verify.
causes: Open-circuit heating element inside the stainless tub
Loose wire at the heater terminal block behind the rear panel
No hot water supply to the hot inlet valve
Heater relay on the main control board not energizing
fixes: Confirm the home hot water faucet is on and delivers warm water at the inlet hoses
Unplug the washer and access the rear panel to inspect heater wire connections
Measure heater resistance with a multimeter—it should not read open/infinite
Run a Sanitize or Heavy Duty cycle and listen for the heater engaging after fill completes

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F22 Error Code
summary: Door lock failed after six attempts
meta_description: Whirlpool washer F22 is a door lock error. Check door tab alignment, clear gasket obstructions, test the lock assembly.
meaning: F22 appears when the door lock motor cannot achieve a locked position after six tries. Whirlpool front-loaders will not fill or spin until the latch confirms closure. You may hear repeated lock attempts before the code displays.
causes: Broken door tab that inserts into the lock hole
Clothing trapped in the door gasket fold preventing full closure
Misaligned strike plate on the cabinet frame
Failed door lock motor or wiring at the DLS harness
fixes: Remove fabric from the gasket fold and press the door until a single lock click is heard
Inspect the plastic tab on the door edge for cracks and replace the door if broken
Power-cycle by unplugging for two minutes, then close the door firmly and restart
Listen at the lock during start—a buzzing with no click points to a failed latch motor
when_to_call_technician: Replace the door lock assembly if the door physically closes but F22 appears every cycle.

If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

title: Whirlpool Washing Machine F24 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool washer F24 is a thermistor error. Check the NTC sensor at the tub bottom, verify wire harness to the control.
meaning: F24 means the tub temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) at the rear bottom of the outer tub sent an out-of-range reading. The washer cannot safely regulate wash heat or cold-water rinses without valid temperature feedback.
causes: Thermistor connector loose at the main control harness
Damaged two-wire harness between tub and control panel
Failed NTC thermistor reading open or shorted
Water intrusion into the sensor connector from a tub seal leak
fixes: Unplug the washer and inspect the thermistor plug near the tub bottom next to the heater
Check continuity of the sensor harness from tub to CCU—repair any frayed wires
Measure thermistor resistance at room temperature against the tech sheet spec for your model
Power-cycle for two minutes and run a cold rinse to see if F24 clears without heat demand

If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most F33 calls are resolved without parts: unplug the washer and tilt or access the bottom panel to reach the drain pump connector. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If F33 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.

title: Whirlpool Dryer F22 Error Code

title: Whirlpool Dryer F24 Error Code
summary: Inlet thermistor open at high-limit thermostat
meta_description: Whirlpool dryer F24 is an open inlet thermistor. Check high-limit thermostat harness, expect ~50K ohms at room temp.
meaning: F24 means the inlet thermistor integrated into the high-limit thermostat assembly reads open. This sensor tracks incoming air temperature before it passes the heating element.
causes: Inlet thermistor wire broken at the high-limit thermostat
Connector vibrated loose on the heater housing
Failed inlet thermistor inside the thermostat assembly
Thermal fuse blowing and taking the thermistor circuit offline
fixes: Unplug the dryer and access the heater housing on the blower outlet
Locate the high-limit thermostat with the inlet thermistor pigtail
Measure thermistor resistance—Duet docs expect roughly 50,000 ohms at room temperature
Replace the high-limit thermostat assembly if the inlet sensor reads open
when_to_call_technician: Gas dryer inlet thermistor replacement sits near the burner tube—use a technician if you are not comfortable working near gas lines.
deep_dive: F24 on Whirlpool dryer units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Whirlpool WED5100HC, Whirlpool WED5620HW, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Inlet thermistor wire broken at the high-limit thermostat is a leading trigger. Start with unplug the dryer and access the heater housing on the blower outlet. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F24 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes F24.

title: Whirlpool Dryer F29 Error Code
summary: Moisture sensor shorted or low resistance
meta_description: Whirlpool dryer F29 means moisture sensor short. Clean bars, inspect harness for shorts, replace sensor strips.
meaning: F29 is the short-circuit counterpart to F28 on Whirlpool dryers. The moisture sensor circuit reads abnormally low resistance, often from contamination or a wiring short at the drum bulkhead.
causes: Water or metal object bridging the two sensor bars
Shorted harness wire inside the drum front panel
Corroded sensor terminals creating a constant low-resistance path
Control board moisture sensor input failure
fixes: Power off and inspect the drum for wet items or metal leaving a film on the bars
Clean both sensor strips until the metal shines evenly
Disconnect the sensor harness and measure bar-to-bar resistance—it should be high when dry
Replace the moisture sensor assembly if resistance stays near zero with dry bars
when_to_call_technician: If new sensor bars test open when dry but F29 persists, the main control board input is likely faulty.
deep_dive: Heating faults such as F29 appear when the Whirlpool dryer control energizes the bake element, broil element, gas valve, or drying heater but does not detect expected temperature rise within the monitoring window. On Whirlpool WED5620HW, Whirlpool WED5100HC, and similar variants, open heating elements, failed igniters on gas dryers and ranges, or welded heater relays on the control board produce similar symptoms.

Water or metal object bridging the two sensor bars should be ruled out first. power off and inspect the drum for wet items or metal leaving a film on the bars. On electric ovens and dryers, test element continuity when cool; on gas appliances verify the ignitor glows for at least 15 seconds before the valve opens. Repeated F29 after confirming heat at the exhaust or oven cavity indicates a sensor or board issue rather than a simple element replacement.
prevention_tips: Preheat the oven only as directed and avoid foil lining the bottom—blocked airflow causes heat faults related to F29.

title: Whirlpool Dryer F41 Error Code

meta_description: Whirlpool dryer F41 points to blower motor failure. Check motor plug, test blower wheel, verify motor control board.
meaning: F41 means the blower motor or its dedicated motor control board is not operating correctly. The dryer needs blower airflow for heat distribution—without it, the control halts the cycle.

Failed blower motor capacitor or winding
Motor control board not sending run voltage

title: Whirlpool Dryer PF Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool dryer PF means power failure. Press Start to resume or Power to clear—check breaker if PF repeats often.
meaning: PF is an informational power-failure code, not a component fault. The dryer detected a loss of line voltage mid-cycle—from a tripped breaker, brief outage, or unplugging—and paused with PF on the display.

Loose power cord at the terminal block causing intermittent contact
User unplugging the dryer before the cycle finished

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher F3E2 Error Code

meta_description: Whirlpool dishwasher F3E2 means shorted OWI/thermistor. Check turbidity sensor wiring, test NTC resistance.

Failed OWI sensor sending a constant low-resistance reading

Dry the connector and inspect for green corrosion on the pins
Measure thermistor resistance—it should not read near zero ohms at room temperature

title: Whirlpool Dishwasher F1E2 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool dishwasher F1E2 means CCU memory fault. Power-cycle one minute, replace main control if code persists.
meaning: F1E2 means the main control board detected corrupted memory or a failed self-check on its EEPROM. The dishwasher may power on but refuse to start cycles or may flash F1E2 immediately.
causes: Power surge corrupting control board memory
Failed EEPROM chip on the main control
Moisture drip on the control board from a tub vent leak
Incorrect control board installed for the model series
fixes: Disconnect power for one full minute at the breaker, then restore
Check for water stains on the control board inside the door after removing the panel
Verify the model tag and compare the board part number to the tech sheet
If F1E2 appears at power-up every time, replace the main electronic control board
when_to_call_technician: Board replacement should use the exact revision listed on your tech sheet—have a technician confirm before purchase.
deep_dive: Whirlpool dishwasher models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F1E2 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Whirlpool WDT750SAKZ, Whirlpool WDF540PADM, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Power surge corrupting control board memory is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, disconnect power for one full minute at the breaker, then restore. If the latch clicks but F1E2 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Whirlpool units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F1E2 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

title: Whirlpool Refrigerator Error Code 22
summary: Pantry compartment sensor 2 out of range
meta_description: Whirlpool fridge code 22 means PC sensor 2 resistance fault. Check module connector, test cold-side thermistor.
meaning: Code 22 means the resistance of pantry compartment (PC) sensor 2 is outside the normal range—above 45K ohms when cold. The ice maker module or UI cannot read accurate temperature for the refrigerated drawer zone.

Open-circuit PC2 thermistor wire in the door harness
Failed PC2 sensor reading too high when chilled
Corroded pins at the module J1 connector from condensation

Unplug the refrigerator and reseat the PC sensor harness at the module
Measure PC2 sensor resistance at the connector against the cold-temperature spec on the tech sheet
Replace the PC2 thermistor if resistance stays above 45K ohms at refrigerator temperature

title: Whirlpool Refrigerator Error Code 30
summary: Ice mold heater not reaching temperature
meta_description: Whirlpool fridge code 30 means mold heater error. Test heater resistance, check mold thermostat, run harvest test.
meaning: Code 30 means the ice mold heater failed to heat the mold to release temperature during harvest. The module timed out waiting for the thermistor to confirm the mold warmed enough to eject cubes.
causes: Open mold heater element inside the ice maker body
Burned heater connection at the ice maker harness
Failed mold thermostat opening too early
Heavy scale insulating the mold from the heater
fixes: Remove the ice maker and inspect heater wires for heat discoloration
Measure mold heater resistance—it should not read open
Run Service Test 58 heater activation and watch for mold surface warming
Replace the ice maker if the heater is open or does not warm during test
when_to_call_technician: Mold heater replacement is part of the ice maker head—service can swap the assembly if harvest repeatedly fails with code 30.
deep_dive: Whirlpool refrigerator models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. 30 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Whirlpool WRX735SDHZ, Whirlpool WRF555SDFZ, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Open mold heater element inside the ice maker body is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, remove the ice maker and inspect heater wires for heat discoloration. If the latch clicks but 30 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Whirlpool units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—30 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when 30 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Whirlpool refrigerator—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for 30; Whirlpool uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting 30; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: Whirlpool Oven F03 Error Code
meta_description: Whirlpool oven F03 means shorted temp sensor. Test RTD for ~1080 ohms at room temp, replace WPW10181986 sensor.
meaning: F03 means the oven temperature sensor (RTD) resistance is abnormally low—a short circuit in the probe or its wiring. The ERC cannot read cavity temperature and disables bake and broil elements as a safety step.

Moisture in the sensor connector after steam from cooking
Harness pinched between the oven back panel and cabinet
