title: Bosch Dishwasher E15 Error Code
summary: AquaStop leak detected—water in the base pan tripped the float
meta_description: Bosch E15 means water in the base pan tripped AquaStop. Remove kick plate, dry float, find the leak on SHPM88Z75N and SHEM63W55N—tilt 30–45° to drain.
meaning: Bosch E15 indicates the AquaStop leak protection system has detected water in the sealed base pan beneath the tub. A polystyrene float disk rises with even a few ounces of water and opens a switch that latches E15 on the display until the pan is dry and the leak source is addressed. The dishwasher will not fill or run pumps while E15 is active. This is not a drain fault (see E24) or a heat fault (see E09)—it is specifically base-pan water from a leak, overflow, or condensation accumulation that exceeded the float threshold.
causes: Loose or slipped clamp on circulation pump outlet hose at the sump assembly
Door gasket tear, pinhole, or food debris gap allowing spray to exit during wash arm pressurization
Inlet valve drip or seep after fill completes, pooling overnight in the base pan
Overfill or suds overflow from incorrect detergent type passing over the door sill into the pan
Cracked sump or tub weld seam seep—rarer but persistent after every cycle
Drain hose connection seep at the pump outlet when the clamp uses a worm gear that was under-tightened at install
Condensation accumulation in unvented cabinet installations combining with a minor seep to trip float over multiple cycles
fixes: Unplug the dishwasher and turn off the hot supply valve under the sink before opening the kick plate.
Remove the toe kick (usually two clip screws or quarter-turn fasteners); locate the polystyrene float in the front base and confirm it is raised—towel-dry the pan until the float drops freely.
Tilt the unit 30–45° toward the front (support the door and use a second person); catch drained water in towels, then level and confirm the float rests down.
Inspect the sump hose clamps visible from the front with a flashlight; tighten quarter-turn or worm clamps clockwise until snug—do not crush plastic barbs.
Run the lower spray arm by hand; remove food from gasket channel and check the bottom six-o'clock gasket area for splits.
Reconnect power, run a Quick wash with the lower rack out, and watch the door bottom and sump during fill and wash for new drips—stop immediately if water reappears in the pan.
If E15 returns without visible spray leak, pull the dishwasher slightly forward and inspect inlet valve and drain pump hose connections at the rear access panel per model service sheet.
when_to_call_technician: Call service when the pan is dry, float resets, and E15 returns on the next fill with no visible door or hose leak. Internal tub seam seep, sump crack, or inlet valve body failure requires cabinet pull and part replacement. On SHPM88Z75N integrated units, professional removal prevents countertop damage. Provide model SHX78B75UC or exact variant for correct gasket and valve parts.
deep_dive: Bosch SHPM88Z75N, SHEM63W55N, and SHX78B75UC integrate AquaStop as a passive mechanical float—no electronic leak sensor in the pan on most Ascenta and 500/800 series units. When the kick plate is removed, the white float assembly sits in the front-left base area on many models. Water from a loose circulation hose clamp, a split door gasket, a failed inlet valve drip after fill, or an overfill that escapes the tub edge lands in the pan and triggers E15 within seconds to minutes depending on leak rate.

E15 latches until power is removed and the pan is dried. Simply canceling the cycle rarely clears it if the float remains buoyant. Technicians tilt the entire dishwasher 30–45 degrees toward the front-right corner (model-dependent) to drain pooled water through a designed channel, then towel-dry the pan and reset the float. Homeowners can perform controlled tilt with the unit disconnected from power and supply, but two people are recommended—Bosch units are heavy and tilting without support can stress door hinges.

Finding the leak source separates a one-hour fix from repeat E15. Common SHPM88Z75N failures include the circulation pump outlet hose clamp at the sump, the water inlet valve body seep after fill shutoff, and spray arm hub seals that leak only under pressure during the wash phase. Inspect with a flashlight after tilting and drying: run a short fill test with the lower rack removed and watch the sump area, door bottom, and hose junctions for fresh droplets.

Do not bypass the float or disable AquaStop. E15 is doing its job—running the dishwasher with water in the pan risks floor damage and electrical hazard at base-level wiring. If no external leak is visible after drying, check for suds overflow from non-HE detergent; excessive foam can spill over the door lip into the pan and mimic a mechanical leak on the first occurrence.
prevention_tips: Use only Bosch-recommended or HE dishwasher detergent to prevent suds overflow that trips AquaStop without a mechanical leak.
Inspect sump hose clamps after any service that disturbed the lower wash arm or pump area.
Leave the kick plate off briefly after E15 recovery and run one supervised cycle to catch slow seep before reinstalling cosmetic panels.
part_cost_estimate: Door gasket: $40–80; inlet valve: $60–120; circulation hose clamp kit: $5–15

title: Bosch Dishwasher E24 Error Code
summary: Drain fault—water not leaving the sump within the programmed window
meta_description: Bosch E24 drain fault: clean twist-lock bottom filter, seat pump cover (E:25 bulletin), check disposal knockout on SHPM65W55N and SHE3AR75UC.
meaning: Bosch E24 is a drain timeout fault. After wash or rinse, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval while the control expects the sump to empty. If water remains—because the pump cannot move it, the filter is blocked, the pump cover is unseated, or the drain hose is obstructed—E24 displays and the cycle halts. E24 is distinct from E15 (base pan leak) and E09 (heat pump heater fault). On SHPM65W55N, SHE3AR75UC, and SHX33A05UC platforms, most E24 cases resolve at the bottom filter and pump cover without replacing the drain pump.
causes: Twist-lock bottom filter clogged with seeds, glass shards, or pasta starch blocking flow to the pump
Pump cover under the filter not fully seated to the click position per Bosch E:25 bulletin
Garbage disposal knockout plug still in place on new kitchen installations
Kinked or sagging drain hose creating a water trap in the cabinet run
Air gap or high-loop obstruction at the countertop penetration
Drain pump impeller jammed by debris that passed an incorrectly seated pump cover
Failed drain pump motor—open winding or seized bearing—after repeated jam events
fixes: Cancel the cycle, unplug the dishwasher, and remove the bottom rack to access the tub floor filter.
Turn the cylindrical filter counterclockwise to unlock, lift out, rinse under running water, and scrub the mesh with a soft brush.
Remove the pump cover beneath the filter cavity; inspect the impeller for debris; rotate the impeller clockwise by finger—it should spin freely.
Reinstall the pump cover aligning mark to mark, push down, and rotate clockwise until a distinct click confirms full seat per E:25.
Under the sink, disconnect the drain hose at the disposal or tailpiece; poke the disposal inlet with a screwdriver handle to confirm knockout is open; run water through the hose into a bucket.
Verify the drain hose high loop peaks at least under the counter underside with no kinks; re-secure with zip ties away from sharp cabinet screws.
Run a quick rinse-only cycle and listen for steady pump hum and water flow at the sink drain; if E24 returns, access the drain pump connector beneath and ohm the motor per service spec.
when_to_call_technician: Contact a technician when E24 persists after filter, cover, disposal, and hose verification, and the impeller spins freely by hand but the pump does not move water during drain. Pump motor replacement or sump disassembly may be required. If glass was heard grinding before E24, mention possible impeller damage—running repeated cycles can crack the pump housing on SHPM65W55N units.
deep_dive: Bosch dishwashers use a twist-lock cylindrical filter in the tub floor. Beneath it sits a pump cover that must click fully seated—service bulletin E:25 documents E24 and related drain faults when the cover is rotated even slightly off index after filter cleaning. Homeowners often remove the filter correctly but reinstall the cover one notch short of the locked detent, leaving a gap that lets food bypass the impeller guard and jam the pump.

Garbage disposal knockouts are the classic new-install E24: the dishwasher drain hose connects to the disposal inlet, but the factory plug was never punched out. Water enters the hose during drain but hits the solid plug; the pump hums, the sump stays full, E24 appears within 30–90 seconds. Always verify disposal drain port flow with a bucket test independent of the dishwasher before ordering parts.

The drain hose high loop to the underside of the countertop prevents siphon backflow but must not kink behind the cabinet. Bosch specifies routing without low spots that hold water and restrict pulse draining. Inspect the air gap (if equipped) for clog at the cap—California and some codes require air gaps that clog with grease and mimic pump failure.

When the filter, cover, disposal port, and hose are clear but E24 persists, listen at the drain pump during the fault: silence suggests failed pump motor or loose harness at the sump; humming with no water movement suggests impeller jam from glass or seed. Access the pump from beneath after removing the toe kick and side panels per model—SHX33A05UC compact units require careful orientation to avoid stressing drain hose barbs.
prevention_tips: After every filter clean, rotate the pump cover until the click—partial seat is the top cause of repeat E24.
Scrape plates before loading; seeds and lemon pits are the most common impeller jams on Bosch sump pumps.
On new installs, confirm garbage disposal knockout removal before connecting the dishwasher drain hose.

title: Bosch Dishwasher E09 Error Code
summary: Heat pump internal heater fault—flow-through heater in circulation pump assembly
meta_description: Bosch E09 heat pump heater fault on SHX68T52UC: reset breaker 30s; flow-through heater ~20–30Ω, part 12008381 $150–250.
meaning: Bosch E09 indicates a fault in the heat pump's internal heater—the flow-through heating element integrated in the circulation pump assembly on zeolite/heat-pump Bosch dishwashers such as SHX68T52UC/09. The control monitors heater circuit resistance and current; readings outside spec latch E09. This is not E15 (AquaStop leak) or E24 (drain)—it is specifically the pump-mounted heater used to boost wash water temperature efficiently. Power reset helps only when a transient glitch occurred; persistent E09 usually requires heater or circulation pump module service.
causes: Open heating element inside circulation pump module—no continuity across heater terminals
Heater short to ground from failed element insulation inside pump assembly
Loose or corroded heater connector at pump after E15 base pan wetting event
Circulation pump module 12008381 internal failure combining pump and heater circuits
Main control heat relay not delivering voltage—after heater ohms verified good
Scale buildup on heater surface causing localized overheat and thermal cutout—on hard water units
Power surge damaging heater or control board heat driver circuit
fixes: Turn off dishwasher breaker for 30 seconds; restore power; run empty Quick wash on hottest setting to test for transient E09.
If E09 returns during heat step, unplug unit and remove toe kick and side access per model sheet to expose circulation pump.
Disconnect heater harness at pump module; measure resistance across heater pins—expect ~20–30 Ω; open or near zero indicates pump/heater replacement.
Inspect connector boots for corrosion or moisture; dry thoroughly if E15 occurred recently before condemning module.
Order circulation pump with integrated heater 12008381 matched to SHX68T52UC serial; drain sump before removal.
Twist-lock or bolt-remove old pump per service guide; transfer sump gasket if not included; install new module without pinching heater wires.
Run sanitize cycle empty; confirm E09 cleared and no leak at pump junction before pushing dishwasher back into cabinet.
when_to_call_technician: E09 on integrated Bosch heat-pump units often requires cabinet removal and pump module replacement—schedule service if ohms confirm heater failure or if you cannot safely access the circulation pump. Module 12008381 is costly; verify warranty and serial-specific part before purchase. Mention SHX68T52UC/09 and any recent E15 history for connector corrosion check.
deep_dive: Heat-pump Bosch models route water through a circulation pump that includes an inline heater element (part 12008381, typically $150–250). Expected resistance at the heater connector is roughly 20–30 Ω at room temperature depending on revision. Open circuit means burned element; very low ohms suggests short to ground inside the pump module. E09 stores when the board detects either condition during the heat phase of wash or sanitize cycles.

Before ordering the pump/heater module, reset household breaker for the dishwasher circuit—full 30 seconds off—to clear latched faults from power spikes. Run a hot sanitize cycle empty and monitor: E09 in the first heat window confirms hardware fault; E09 only after long run may indicate wiring heat expansion intermittent at the pump connector beneath the unit.

Access requires tipping or removing the dishwasher from the cabinet on integrated SHX68T52UC installs. The circulation pump sits at the sump; heater leads route through a moisture-resistant boot. Inspect for water ingress at the connector—AquaStop events (E15) that wet the base can corrode heater pins and mimic E09 until dried. Do not run heat tests with visible water in the base pan.

Related H01 (delay start) and E24 may appear in history from owner cancel attempts during fault. Document serial and production week when ordering 12008381—Bosch revised pump modules across SHX68T52UC/09 production. Professional install includes checking fill water temperature at sink—if inlet water is already hot, some E09 edge cases relate to thermistor logic rather than open heater; still verify ohms first.
prevention_tips: Resolve E15 base pan leaks promptly—moisture at the pump heater connector causes repeat E09 corrosion faults.
Use Bosch-recommended detergent and periodic machine clean to limit scale on heat-pump heater surfaces in hard water.
Install surge protection on the dishwasher circuit to reduce heat driver damage after storms.
part_cost_estimate: Circulation pump/heater 12008381: $150–250; connector harness if corroded: $30–60

title: Bosch Dishwasher E14 Error Code
summary: Flow meter not detecting inlet water
meta_description: Bosch E14 means water is not entering correctly. Open supply, clean inlet filters, and verify 10 L/min flow at the tap.
meaning: E14 means flow meter failure or inadequate fill—the reed-switch flow sensor at the inlet did not register expected pulses while the inlet valve was open. Cycles may stop before wash begins or mid-fill with E14.
causes: Closed or partially closed hot water supply valve
Inlet hose kinked when the dishwasher was pushed under the counter
Clogged inlet filter screen at the valve body
Failed flow meter turbine or inlet valve solenoid
fixes: Confirm the supply valve under the sink is fully open
Inspect the inlet hose for kinks along its length to the valve
Shut off water, disconnect the hose, and rinse the mesh screen in the valve port
Measure roughly 10 liters per minute at the disconnected hose per Bosch spec
when_to_call_technician: Strong flow at the hose with recurring E14 requires flow meter or inlet valve replacement inside the kick panel.
deep_dive: Bosch dishwasher models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. E14 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Bosch SHPM88Z75N, Bosch SHE53B75UC, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Closed or partially closed hot water supply valve is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, confirm the supply valve under the sink is fully open. If the latch clicks but E14 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Bosch units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—E14 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when E14 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Bosch dishwasher—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for E14; Bosch uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting E14; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: Bosch Dishwasher E01 Error Code
summary: Heat pump motor circuit fault at control board
meta_description: Bosch E01 indicates a heat pump motor circuit error on the main board. Reset breaker once; persistent E01 needs Bosch service.
meaning: E01 means a heat pump motor circuit fault at the main control board—the drive that circulates refrigerant in CrystalDry heat-pump dishwashers is not responding. The dishwasher may not start or may stop during dry phase with E01.
causes: Heat pump motor driver failure on the power module
Loose connector at the heat pump assembly after shipping
Power surge damaging the motor circuit on the board
Heat pump motor seized from lack of maintenance descaling
fixes: Reset breaker thirty seconds per Bosch official procedure
Ensure the dishwasher is on a dedicated 15A or 20A circuit without extension cords
Run one empty Normal cycle after reset before loading dishes
Stop using Sanitize/CrystalDry until E01 clears on a basic cycle
when_to_call_technician: E01 after reset requires Bosch-certified heat pump or power module service—boards may need programming.
deep_dive: When E01 appears on a Bosch dishwasher, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Bosch SHPM88Z75N, Bosch SHX878ZD5N, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most E01 calls are resolved without parts: reset breaker thirty seconds per bosch official procedure. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If E01 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use dishwashers to prevent E01 from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when E01 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Bosch dishwasher—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for E01; Bosch uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.

title: Bosch Dishwasher E16 Error Code
summary: Water not entering the tub correctly
meta_description: Bosch E16 means fill system fault—water cannot enter properly. Check inlet hose, supply valve, and 10 L/min flow rate.
meaning: E16 (often paired with E17 on some firmware) means water is not entering the dishwasher correctly—the fill system timed out or the level stayed too low after the inlet valve opened. Distinct from E14 which specifically flags the flow meter.
causes: Kinked or damaged inlet hose under the sink
Supply valve closed or flow restrictor installed upstream
Inlet valve screen clogged with sediment
Fill phase aborted by air gap siphon effect on improper install
fixes: Inspect the full inlet hose path from valve to dishwasher for kinks
Open the supply valve fully and remove any aftermarket flow restrictors
Disconnect the hose and confirm roughly 10 liters per minute flow into a bucket
Reset breaker thirty seconds after verifying supply
when_to_call_technician: Confirmed strong inlet flow with recurring E16 requires inlet valve or fill pressure switch service.
deep_dive: E16 on Bosch dishwasher units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Bosch SHPM65Z55N, Bosch SHE53B75UC, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Kinked or damaged inlet hose under the sink is a leading trigger. Start with inspect the full inlet hose path from valve to dishwasher for kinks. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated E16 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes E16.

title: Bosch Refrigerator E2 Error Code

meaning: E2 on Bosch built-in and counter-depth refrigerators indicates the freezer compartment temperature sensor is faulty—the thermistor in the freezer section stopped sending valid resistance readings, so the board cannot regulate compressor and defrost cycles accurately.

If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

title: Bosch Refrigerator E7 Error Code
meta_description: Bosch E7 means evaporator fan failure. Listen for fan after reset; manual defrost if iced, then replace fan if silent.
meaning: E7 means the evaporator fan motor did not provide speed feedback—the fan that circulates cold air from the freezer coil to both compartments stopped or the board lost its signal. Warm fridge with cold freezer floor often appears before E7.
causes: Ice buildup locking the evaporator fan blade
Failed BLDC evaporator motor after years of run time
Defrost system fault refreezing the coil weekly
Loose fan connector behind the freezer rear panel

deep_dive: Bosch refrigerator models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. E7 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Bosch B36CL80ENS, Bosch B36CD50SNS, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Ice buildup locking the evaporator fan blade is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, reset breaker thirty seconds and listen for fan whir in the freezer. If the latch clicks but E7 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Bosch units.

title: Bosch Oven F41 Error Code

meaning: F41 means the upper or single oven motorized latch will not lock—the latch motor ran but switches never confirmed a locked position, so self-clean cannot start. Standard baking may still work on some models.

Latch motor failed after prior F38 overcurrent events
Door slightly misaligned on hinge cam wear

Reseat P24 at the power board if accessible from the rear service panel
Close the door and listen for latch motor whir during self-clean attempt
Avoid self-clean until F41 clears—forced heat without lock is unsafe

title: Bosch Oven F45 Error Code
summary: Door latch switches stuck or defective
meta_description: Bosch F45 means latch switches read wrong. Inspect switch actuators; replace latch assembly if switches fail manual test.
meaning: F45 means the electronic control detected door latch sensor switches stuck or defective—the motor may move but microswitches on the latch assembly do not change state, so the board refuses heat modes that require a confirmed lock or unlock position.

Common wire pinched between latch bracket and chassis

Clean grease from the latch cam area with degreaser on a cloth, not dripping liquid into switches
Reseat P24 connector at the power board
Retry standard Bake before self-clean to confirm basic switch feedback

title: Maytag Washing Machine F0E2 Error Code

meaning: F0E2 is Maytag's oversuds fault. The pressure sensor sees foam preventing the basket from reaching spin speed, or suds rising faster than the control can flush away. The washer may extend rinse time or stop before final spin.

title: Maytag Washing Machine F0E3 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F0E3 overload fault. Remove items, check for socks between tubs, verify basket spins freely by hand.
meaning: F0E3 means the load exceeds the tub capacity or the basket is mechanically bound. Maytag top-loaders run an overload recovery routine that redistributes water and pauses agitation until the basket can move.

Small items trapped under the Power Impeller
Cracked driven hub under the impeller preventing engagement

title: Maytag Washing Machine F0E4 Error Code
summary: Spin limited by high rinse water temperature
meta_description: Maytag F0E4 limits spin to 500 RPM when rinse water exceeds 105°F. Check reversed fill hoses and inlet thermistor.
meaning: F0E4 appears when rinse water temperature reads 105°F or higher. Maytag reduces final spin to about 500 RPM to protect seals and bearings from thermal shock during high-speed extraction.
causes: Hot and cold supply hoses connected to the wrong faucets
Water heater delivering above 120°F at the laundry tap
Inlet thermistor in the mixing valve reading incorrectly
Stuck hot-side inlet valve bleeding hot water during cold rinse
fixes: Trace each fill hose to confirm cold connects to the blue valve and hot to red
Run the nearest tap and verify hot water is not scalding before starting a wash
Test inlet valves by unplugging mid-fill—water should stop within seconds
Select Cold Rinse on the next cycle after correcting hose routing
when_to_call_technician: Correct hose routing with lukewarm tap water that still triggers F0E4 needs inlet valve and thermistor replacement.

title: Maytag Washing Machine F0E5 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F0E5 off-balance error. Redistribute wet laundry, level the washer, inspect suspension rods.
meaning: F0E5 means the tub moved excessively during spin. Maytag's Advanced Vibration Control pauses the cycle when weight clusters on one side rather than allow the cabinet to walk across the floor.
causes: Heavy wet jeans or towels piled on one side of the basket
Worn suspension rods allowing multiple bounces after a push test
Washer not level—one front leg shorter than the others
Washing a single bath mat without balancing items
fixes: Pause, open the lid, and spread wet items around the basket perimeter
Push the basket down at 3 and 9 o'clock—it should rebound once, not oscillate
Level all four feet with a bubble level and tighten lock nuts
Add towels when washing one heavy article alone
when_to_call_technician: Replace all four suspension rods as a set if F0E5 returns on evenly distributed full loads after leveling.
deep_dive: Maytag washing machine models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F0E5 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Maytag MVW7232HW, Maytag MVW6200KW, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Heavy wet jeans or towels piled on one side of the basket is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, pause, open the lid, and spread wet items around the basket perimeter. If the latch clicks but F0E5 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Maytag units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F0E5 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when F0E5 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Maytag washing machine—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for F0E5; Maytag uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting F0E5; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

meta_description: Maytag F0E8 water ring fault. Run Drain & Spin, check drain hose height, clear pump filter.

title: Maytag Washing Machine F2E1 Error Code
summary: Stuck button on the control panel
meta_description: Maytag F2E1 HMI stuck key fault. Clean the touch panel, power-cycle—replace the interface if a key reads pressed constantly.
meaning: F2E1 means the Human Machine Interface reports a button held down continuously. The ACU will not start a cycle while it thinks Start, Power, or a cycle key is permanently pressed.
causes: Moisture or detergent residue bridging touch pads
Cracked HMI overlay causing a stuck trace
Liquid spill into the control panel membrane
Failed HMI board behind the knob and touch assembly
fixes: Wipe the control surface with a barely damp cloth and dry thoroughly
Unplug two minutes and press each button once after power returns
Avoid pressing multiple keys during power-up
Listen for beeps when no key is touched—indicates a failed HMI
when_to_call_technician: Replace the HMI assembly when F2E1 appears with a dry, untouched panel.
deep_dive: F2E1 on Maytag washing machine units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Maytag MVW6200KW, Maytag MVW7232HW, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Moisture or detergent residue bridging touch pads is a leading trigger. Start with wipe the control surface with a barely damp cloth and dry thoroughly. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F2E1 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes F2E1.

summary: Long fill—water level not rising in time
meta_description: Maytag F8E1 long fill error. Open supply valves, clean inlet screens, verify drain hose air gap.
meaning: F8E1 appears when the tub does not reach the expected water level within about ten minutes. The ACU sees valves open but the pressure switch never reports a rise—one of the most common Maytag top-load codes.

title: Maytag Dryer F02 Error Code
summary: Key stuck on the user interface
meta_description: Maytag F02 stuck button on dryer console. Clean the panel, power-cycle, replace UI if keys read pressed constantly.
meaning: F02 means the console detects a keypad or touch button held continuously. The dryer will not start a cycle while it thinks a selection key is pressed.
causes: Static discharge locking a touch pad trace
Spilled liquid on the console membrane
Cracked overlay shorting adjacent pads
Failed user interface board
fixes: Wipe the console dry with a soft cloth
Unplug two minutes and touch each button once after restart
Avoid leaning on the console during operation
Replace the UI if F02 shows with no keys touched
when_to_call_technician: UI board replacement is straightforward but requires matching the part to your console style—order by model number.
deep_dive: Maytag dryer models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F02 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Maytag MEDB855DC, Maytag MEDC465HW, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Static discharge locking a touch pad trace is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, wipe the console dry with a soft cloth. If the latch clicks but F02 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Maytag units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F02 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

deep_dive: When F05 appears on a Maytag dryer, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Maytag MEDC465HW, Maytag MGDC465HW, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most F05 calls are resolved without parts: close the door firmly and listen for the drum light to extinguish. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If F05 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use dryers to prevent F05 from lint and food debris buildup.

title: Maytag Dryer F11 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F11 shorted key on dryer console. Dry the panel, replace UI if a key reads shorted without touch.
meaning: F11 means the UI sees a continuous short on one keypad line—different from F02 stuck key which is a logic state. Liquid spills or board failure are common triggers.
causes: Water from a nearby sink splashed on the console
Shorted membrane under the Start pad
Liquid fabric softener on the touch surface
Failed UI board with internal short
fixes: Power off and dry the console thoroughly for several hours
Inspect for visible moisture under the plastic overlay
Unplug and leave the door open to ventilate the control area

when_to_call_technician: UI replacement is the typical fix when drying does not clear F11.
deep_dive: Maytag dryer models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F11 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Maytag MEDB855DC, Maytag MED6230HW, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Water from a nearby sink splashed on the console is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, power off and dry the console thoroughly for several hours. If the latch clicks but F11 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Maytag units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F11 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F4E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F4E2 wash pump fault. Check for glass in the sump, verify pump connector, listen for hum at wash.
meaning: F4E2 means the main wash circulation pump failed to start or report correct feedback. Dishes stay dirty when wash arms do not pressurize.
causes: Glass shard or seed lodged in the pump impeller
Loose pump connector under the tub
Seized pump bearing from hard water scale
Control relay not sending voltage to the pump
fixes: Cancel the cycle and remove standing water with a towel
Remove the lower spray arm and filter to inspect the sump
Spin the impeller by hand when power is off—it should turn freely
Restart a normal wash and listen for pump hum in the first two minutes
when_to_call_technician: Pump hums without water movement or F4E2 with a free impeller requires circulation pump replacement.
deep_dive: F4E2 on Maytag dishwasher units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Maytag MDB4949SKZ, Maytag MDB8989SHZ, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Glass shard or seed lodged in the pump impeller is a leading trigger. Start with cancel the cycle and remove standing water with a towel. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F4E2 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F5E2 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F5E2 door switch fault. Adjust strike, inspect latch microswitch, replace if door closed but code remains.
meaning: F5E2 means the control never saw the door switch close. Maytag dishwashers block fill and heat until the latch confirms the door is sealed.
causes: Misaligned door strike not pressing the switch
Broken latch hook on the door
Switch lever stuck from detergent buildup
Wire broken in the door harness at the hinge
fixes: Close the door firmly until the latch clicks
Inspect the strike plate on the tub for looseness
Clean the latch receiver with a damp cloth
Run a cycle and listen for the fill valve only after a confirmed latch click
when_to_call_technician: Door switch or latch assembly replacement when the door appears shut but F5E2 repeats.
deep_dive: When F5E2 appears on a Maytag dishwasher, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Maytag MDB4949SKZ, Maytag MDB7759AWM, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most F5E2 calls are resolved without parts: close the door firmly until the latch clicks. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If F5E2 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use dishwashers to prevent F5E2 from lint and food debris buildup.

title: Maytag Dishwasher F5E1 Error Code
summary: Float switch open—suds or overflow path
meta_description: Maytag F5E1 float switch fault. Check for suds overflow, inspect float under the tub pan.
meaning: F5E1 means the float switch circuit read open when the control expected continuity. Suds overflow or a stuck float can trigger this safety code.
causes: Excess suds lifting the float in the pan
Float stuck in the up position from debris
Open wire to the float switch
Actual water in the base pan from a hose leak
fixes: Use only dishwasher-specific detergent at the marked fill line
Remove the toe kick and check the float moves freely down
Dry any water in the base pan with towels
Run a rinse cycle after clearing suds
when_to_call_technician: Water returns to the pan after drying—find the leak at inlet valve, door gasket, or pump seal before resetting.
deep_dive: Maytag dishwasher models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F5E1 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Maytag MDB4949SKZ, Maytag MDB7759AWM, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Excess suds lifting the float in the pan is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, use only dishwasher-specific detergent at the marked fill line. If the latch clicks but F5E1 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Maytag units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F5E1 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most 22 calls are resolved without parts: unplug and remove freezer shelves to access the back panel. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If 22 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use refrigerators to prevent 22 from lint and food debris buildup.

title: Maytag Refrigerator Error Code 24
meta_description: Maytag fridge code 24 defrost thermostat fault. Test bimetal thermostat on evaporator coil.
meaning: Code 24 means the defrost thermostat did not close when the heater energized. The board interprets this as a failed defrost temperature path.
causes: Defrost thermostat stuck open from age
Poor contact at the thermostat clamp on the coil
Thermostat wire broken at the evaporator bend
Control board not sending heat long enough to close the stat
fixes: Locate the round bimetal thermostat clipped to the evaporator
Test for continuity at freezer temperature—should open near 50°F
Reseat the harness at the thermostat
Force a manual defrost through service mode if supported
when_to_call_technician: Replace the defrost thermostat W10384183 family part when it never closes during a forced defrost.
deep_dive: Maytag refrigerator models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. 24 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Maytag MFI2570FEZ, Maytag MFF2055FRZ, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Defrost thermostat stuck open from age is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, locate the round bimetal thermostat clipped to the evaporator. If the latch clicks but 24 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Maytag units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—24 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

summary: Main control to UI serial link fault
meta_description: Maytag fridge 36 communication error between boards. Reseat all door and ceiling harnesses.
meaning: Code 36 is a broader communication fault between the main machine control and distributed boards—UI, ice maker, or damper modules.
causes: Corroded CAN or serial connector at the main board
Substitute control with incompatible software
Intermittent neutral causing board resets
Failed main control UART driver
fixes: Unplug 24 hours if recent lightning—then reseat every harness
Verify all connectors at the main control in the ceiling
Check for error codes stored in combination with 35
Record model and serial before ordering boards
when_to_call_technician: Code 36 after full harness service often requires main control and matched UI replacement.
deep_dive: When 36 appears on a Maytag refrigerator, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Maytag MFI2570FEZ, Maytag MFF2055FRZ, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most 36 calls are resolved without parts: unplug 24 hours if recent lightning—then reseat every harness. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If 36 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use refrigerators to prevent 36 from lint and food debris buildup.
Record when 36 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Maytag refrigerator—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for 36; Maytag uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting 36; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: Maytag Oven F03 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag oven F03 RTD sensor open. Check probe connector behind range, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: F03 on Maytag wall ovens and ranges means the oven temperature sensor (RTD) circuit is open. The board cannot regulate bake or broil without a valid probe signal.
causes: Sensor plug loose behind the rear panel
Broken sensor wire inside the oven cavity routing
Failed RTD probe internally open
Connector corrosion from steam at the broil element
fixes: Shut off power at the breaker
Remove the rear lower panel and reseat the sensor harness
Measure probe resistance—about 1080 ohms at room temperature on many models
Replace the probe if resistance reads infinite
when_to_call_technician: New probe with correct ohms but persistent F03 indicates main clock or ERC wiring service.

title: Maytag Oven F04 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F04 shorted oven sensor. Inspect probe for bare wire touching cavity, replace RTD if shorted.
meaning: F04 means the oven temperature sensor reads a short circuit. The control disables heat to prevent uncontrolled temperature rise.
causes: Sensor wire pinched against the oven liner
Moisture shorting the connector after steam cleaning
Failed probe with internal short
Metal braid touching terminal screws
fixes: Disconnect power and inspect the sensor wire route through the cavity
Dry the connector thoroughly if wet
Measure resistance—near zero ohms indicates a shorted probe
Install a new W10181986-style probe matched to your model
when_to_call_technician: F04 with a disconnected probe means a harness short—trace wiring to the ERC.
deep_dive: F04 on Maytag oven units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Maytag MEW7630FZ, Maytag MER8800FZ, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Sensor wire pinched against the oven liner is a leading trigger. Start with disconnect power and inspect the sensor wire route through the cavity. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F04 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes F04.
Record when F04 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Maytag oven—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for F04; Maytag uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting F04; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

Listen for latch motor movement when selecting self-clean
Verify the door closes flush against the gasket

title: Maytag Oven F06 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F06 door switch error. Test latch switches, verify lock engages before clean cycle.
meaning: F06 means the door lock switch circuit failed verification. The ERC detected an unexpected open or short in the lock position feedback.
causes: Latch switch stuck from carbon after clean cycles
Switch harness unplugged during prior service
Failed dual-switch latch assembly
ERC not reading switch bounce correctly
fixes: Power off and manually move the latch to feel for binding
Inspect the two microswitches on the latch motor
Reseat the latch harness at the ERC
Run a short bake at 350°F before attempting self-clean again

title: Maytag Oven F09 Error Code
meta_description: Maytag F09 over-temperature fault. Shut off breaker, test sensor and ERC—do not use until cleared.
meaning: F09 is a safety over-temperature fault. The ERC detected oven temperature above the safe limit—often from a welded relay or failed sensor reading low.
causes: Bake relay stuck closed on the ERC
Sensor detached giving false cold reading
Runaway clean cycle with latch fault
External heat source too close to the range
fixes: Turn off the breaker immediately and leave the door closed until cool
Inspect the sensor mounting in the upper oven cavity
Do not restart until F09 cause is identified
Test bake element for continuity to ground when power is off
when_to_call_technician: F09 after cooldown with a good sensor strongly suggests ERC relay failure—replace the control board.
deep_dive: Maytag oven models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F09 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Maytag MEW7630FZ, Maytag MER8800FZ, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Bake relay stuck closed on the ERC is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, turn off the breaker immediately and leave the door closed until cool. If the latch clicks but F09 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Maytag units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F09 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when F09 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Maytag oven—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for F09; Maytag uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting F09; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

meaning: F10 indicates the oven heated without proportional control—similar to F09 on many Maytag platforms. The ERC shuts down all heat outputs.

Measure bake element resistance and ground leakage
Verify the sensor reads rising resistance as the cavity cools
Replace sensor first if readings are erratic

title: Frigidaire Washing Machine EF11 Error Code
summary: Heating circuit or NTC wash temperature fault
meta_description: Frigidaire EF11 heater error. Verify warm water at tap, test wash NTC and heating element.
meaning: EF11 means the washer could not heat water to the selected temperature—common on models with internal heaters or strict temperature monitoring.
causes: Household water heater set too low
Open heating element circuit
Wash NTC thermistor detached
Tripped thermal cutout on heater can

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Household water heater set too low is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, run the hot tap 30 seconds before starting a warm cycle. After correcting mechanical issues, power off and dry panel hours. title: Frigidaire Refrigerator HD Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire HD display error. Reseat ribbon at dispenser UI, dry connectors.
meaning: HD indicates the dispenser or console UI lost communication with the main board.

deep_dive: When HD appears on a Frigidaire refrigerator, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Frigidaire FRSS2623AS, Frigidaire GRFS2853AF, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most HD calls are resolved without parts: unplug and reseat dispenser ui ribbon. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Surge is a leading trigger. Start with breaker off five minutes. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. title: Frigidaire Oven F17 Error Code
meta_description: Frigidaire F17 probe error. Seat probe fully, check jack wiring to ERC.
meaning: F17 appears on probe-equipped models when the meat probe circuit is open or shorted.
causes: Probe not fully inserted
Damaged probe cable
Loose jack wires
Damaged probe tip
fixes: Insert probe until seated
Test with known-good probe
Inspect jack behind console
Use timed bake as workaround
when_to_call_technician: Good probe with F17 at jack needs receptacle or ERC repair.
deep_dive: Frigidaire oven models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F17 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Frigidaire FGEH3047VF, Frigidaire FPEH3077RF, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Probe not fully inserted is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, insert probe until seated. If the latch clicks but F17 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Frigidaire units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F17 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when F17 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Frigidaire oven—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for F17; Frigidaire uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F11 Error Code
summary: Communication fault between CCU and MCU
meta_description: Kenmore washer F11 CCU-to-MCU comm error. Reseat MS2 and M13 connectors, check wiring diagram in lower panel packet.
meaning: F11 is the most common Kenmore HE4t and HE5t fault—it means serial data between the Central Control Unit and Motor Control Unit was lost. Rinse and spin may stop mid-cycle when the boards cannot exchange motor commands.
causes: Loose MS2(1,2,3) connector at the CCU after vibration
Corroded pins on the M13 harness at the MCU
Failed CCU serial driver chip
Failed MCU not acknowledging CCU messages
fixes: Unplug and remove the top cover to access connectors labeled MS2 and M13 on the tech diagram
Remove and reseat both harness ends—squeeze female pin gaps if loose
Power-cycle five minutes after reconnecting
Run the same cycle that triggered F11 as a verification test
when_to_call_technician: F11 after reseating both harnesses usually requires CCU or MCU replacement—order by model suffix from the rating plate.
deep_dive: When F11 appears on a Kenmore washing machine, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Kenmore Elite 41382 31102, Kenmore Elite 41952 99111, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most F11 calls are resolved without parts: unplug and remove the top cover to access connectors labeled ms2 and m13 on the tech diagram. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If F11 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use washing machines to prevent F11 from lint and food debris buildup.

title: Kenmore Washing Machine F14 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore washer F14 CCU memory fault. Unplug five minutes, reseat harnesses—replace Central Control Unit if fault returns.
meaning: F14 means the Central Control Unit detected a corrupted EEPROM or memory verification failure. Cycle parameters cannot be stored reliably until the board is reset or replaced.
causes: Power surge corrupting CCU memory
Loose 14-pin harness at the CCU after console service
Moisture on the board from an inlet valve leak
Failed EEPROM chip on the main control assembly
fixes: Unplug five minutes to discharge board capacitors
Remove the top cover and reseat every harness into the CCU
Inspect for burn marks near the line input on the CCU
Restore power and run a short rinse as a functional test
when_to_call_technician: Persistent F14 after harness reseat requires a new Kenmore Central Control Unit matched to your model suffix.
deep_dive: Kenmore washing machine models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F14 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Kenmore Elite 41382 31102, Kenmore Elite 41952 99111, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Power surge corrupting CCU memory is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, unplug five minutes to discharge board capacitors. If the latch clicks but F14 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Kenmore units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F14 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when F14 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Kenmore washing machine—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for F14; Kenmore uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.

title: Kenmore Dryer F11 Error Code
summary: Motor relay stuck on the control board
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F11 motor relay fault. Unplug immediately if drum runs with door open, replace main control.
meaning: F11 means the main control detected a motor relay that would not release. The drum may continue turning when the cycle should be paused—a safety concern on Kenmore electronic dryers.
causes: Welded motor relay contacts on the control board
Shorted motor winding keeping relay energized
Failed relay driver transistor
Moisture bridging relay pins on the board
fixes: Unplug the dryer immediately if the drum runs with the door open
Inspect the motor relay area on the control for heat discoloration
Test motor winding resistance for shorts to ground
Do not use the dryer until F11 is cleared
when_to_call_technician: F11 with a good motor requires main control board replacement—relay repair is not practical in the field.
deep_dive: Kenmore dryer models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. F11 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Kenmore 110.65132410, Kenmore 110.67822600, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Welded motor relay contacts on the control board is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, unplug the dryer immediately if the drum runs with the door open. If the latch clicks but F11 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Kenmore units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—F11 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.
Record when F11 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Kenmore dryer—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.
Keep the model and serial number from the rating plate when ordering parts for F11; Kenmore uses multiple revisions with different harness lengths and board firmware.
Photograph wiring connector orientation before disconnecting any component while troubleshooting F11; reversed plugs on pumps and valves cause immediate repeat faults.

title: Kenmore Dryer F15 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dryer F15 shorted exhaust thermistor. Inspect blower housing wiring, replace thermistor if shorted.
meaning: F15 means the exhaust thermistor reads a short circuit. The control disables heat because it cannot trust exhaust temperature feedback.
causes: Thermistor wires pinched at the blower housing
Water from a vent leak shorting the connector
Failed thermistor with internal short
Metal screw through harness insulation
fixes: Unplug and inspect thermistor wiring along the blower outlet
Dry the connector if moisture is present
Measure resistance—near zero ohms at room temp confirms a short
Replace the thermistor if wiring is intact
when_to_call_technician: New thermistor with immediate F15 may indicate a control board input fault.
deep_dive: F15 on Kenmore dryer units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Kenmore 110.65132410, Kenmore 110.67822600, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Thermistor wires pinched at the blower housing is a leading trigger. Start with unplug and inspect thermistor wiring along the blower outlet. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F15 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes F15.
Record when F15 appears in the cycle and whether it clears after a 60-second power reset on your Kenmore dryer—that detail speeds diagnosis if you call service.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F7 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F7 flow meter fault. Clean turbine housing, test WPW10348269 meter, verify inlet valve opens.
meaning: F7 means the flow meter turbine did not pulse when the inlet valve opened. The control uses this sensor to verify water is actually entering the tub—not just that the valve coil energized.
causes: Mineral buildup blocking the flow meter turbine impeller
Failed flow meter WPW10348269
Inlet valve opening only partially
Debris in the inlet path upstream of the meter
fixes: Remove the flow meter from the inlet valve assembly and rinse the turbine
Blow through the meter housing to confirm the impeller spins freely
Verify full water flow at the inlet hose when the valve is commanded open
Replace the flow meter if the turbine is clear but no pulse is detected
when_to_call_technician: New flow meter and valve with persistent F7 may indicate control board fill logic failure.

F7 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Kenmore 665.13223K901, Kenmore 665.17789K801, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Mineral buildup blocking the flow meter turbine impeller is reported frequently on this code. title: Kenmore Dishwasher F8 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F8 drain fault. Clean filter, check disposal knockout, test drain pump motor.
meaning: F8 means the drain pump ran but water remained in the tub after the drain timeout. Standing water at the end of a cycle or cancel/drain triggers this code on Kenmore 665 dishwashers.
causes: Clogged food filter at the tub bottom
Blocked drain hose at the disposal or air gap
Failed drain pump motor
Garbage disposal knockout plug still installed on new setups
fixes: Remove the lower spray arm and clean the filter assembly thoroughly
Check the drain hose at the disposal—remove knockout plug if recently installed
Verify the drain hose has a high loop or air gap
Listen at drain—humming with no water movement means jammed or failed pump
when_to_call_technician: Clear filter and hose with a silent pump at drain command requires drain pump replacement.
deep_dive: When F8 appears on a Kenmore dishwasher, the control board has confirmed that water did not leave the machine within the programmed drain window. On Kenmore 665.13223K901, Kenmore 665.17789K801, and similar variants, the drain pump runs for a fixed interval—often three to seven minutes on washers and dishwashers—while a pressure switch or turbidity sensor verifies the tub or sump is empty. If water remains, the cycle stops to prevent spin with a full tub or overflow on the next fill.

Most F8 calls are resolved without parts: remove the lower spray arm and clean the filter assembly thoroughly. Restricted drain paths mimic pump failure because the impeller spins against a blocked hose or filter. Document whether the pump hums audibly during the drain step; a humming pump with no water movement points to a clogged filter or kinked hose, while silence may indicate a failed pump motor or loose wiring at the harness.

After clearing blockages, run a short cycle and listen at the drain hose for steady water flow. If F8 returns on the next load with a clean filter and straight hose, measure resistance at the pump motor connector per the service manual for your model before ordering a replacement pump assembly.
prevention_tips: Clean drain filters and hose terminations monthly on high-use dishwashers to prevent F8 from lint and food debris buildup.

title: Kenmore Dishwasher F14 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore dishwasher F14 touchpad fault. Clean panel, test each button, replace UI overlay or assembly.
meaning: F14 means the touchpad membrane or button panel failed self-test. Button presses may not register or the panel may report multiple keys at once.
causes: Touch panel membrane degraded after years of use
Moisture under the overlay from steam migration
Ribbon cable corrosion at the UI connector
Physical damage to the button overlay
fixes: Press each button firmly and release—note any that feel sticky
Dry the control surface and avoid spraying cleaner directly on buttons
Disconnect and clean the ribbon cable contacts
Replace the touchpad overlay or combined UI assembly if buttons don't respond
when_to_call_technician: UI replacement on built-in models requires matching the door panel style to your series.
deep_dive: F14 on Kenmore dishwasher units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Kenmore 665.13223K901, Kenmore 665.17789K801, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Touch panel membrane degraded after years of use is a leading trigger. Start with press each button firmly and release—note any that feel sticky. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F14 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes F14.

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 25

title: Kenmore Refrigerator Error Code 30
summary: Ice mold heater not reaching temperature
meta_description: Kenmore fridge code 30 means mold heater error. Test heater resistance, check mold thermostat, run harvest test.
meaning: Code 30 means the ice mold heater failed to heat the mold to release temperature during harvest. The module timed out waiting for the thermistor to confirm the mold warmed enough to eject cubes.
causes: Open mold heater element inside the ice maker body
Burned heater connection at the ice maker harness
Failed mold thermostat opening too early
Heavy scale insulating the mold from the heater
fixes: Remove the ice maker and inspect heater wires for heat discoloration
Measure mold heater resistance—it should not read open
Run Service Test 58 heater activation and watch for mold surface warming
Replace the ice maker if the heater is open or does not warm during test
when_to_call_technician: Mold heater replacement is part of the ice maker head—service can swap the assembly if harvest repeatedly fails with code 30.
deep_dive: Kenmore refrigerator models lock the door or lid before energizing fill, heat, or high-speed spin. 30 means the latch switch never sent a "locked" signal to the main control, or the lock motor did not complete its travel. On Kenmore 106.51133210, Kenmore 106.62562101, and similar variants, clothing caught in the gasket, a misaligned strike, or a failed latch assembly are the most common mechanical causes—not the board itself.

Inspect the door gasket perimeter and strike alignment before replacing the latch. Open mold heater element inside the ice maker body is reported frequently on this code. After correcting mechanical issues, remove the ice maker and inspect heater wires for heat discoloration. If the latch clicks but 30 persists, the door lock switch or wiring harness to the control board likely needs testing with a multimeter.
Do not force the door open during an active cycle; wait for the lock to release when the board de-energizes the lock motor—usually two to three minutes after canceling power on most Kenmore units.
prevention_tips: Wipe the door gasket weekly and avoid overloading items against the seal—30 often starts as a slow leak or latch misalignment.

title: Kenmore Oven F03 Error Code
meta_description: Kenmore oven F03 RTD sensor open. Check probe connector behind range, measure resistance at room temp.
meaning: F03 on Kenmore 790-series ranges means the oven temperature sensor (RTD) circuit is open. The electronic range control cannot regulate bake or broil without a valid probe signal.
causes: Sensor plug loose behind the rear panel
Broken sensor wire inside the oven cavity routing
Failed RTD probe internally open
Connector corrosion from steam at the broil element
fixes: Shut off power at the breaker
Remove the rear lower panel and reseat the sensor harness
Measure probe resistance—about 1080 ohms at room temperature on many models
Replace the probe if resistance reads infinite
when_to_call_technician: New probe with correct ohms but persistent F03 indicates ERC wiring service.
deep_dive: F03 on Kenmore oven units indicates the drive system did not meet expected performance. Direct-drive washers monitor rotor position with a hall sensor; dryers and dishwashers track motor current draw. When the drum or spray arm fails to turn, the inverter or motor relay shuts down to prevent burned windings. Kenmore Elite 790.75903, Kenmore 790.46187300, and similar variants may show this fault after an overloaded tub or a foreign object jam.

Sensor plug loose behind the rear panel is a leading trigger. Start with shut off power at the breaker. Rotate the drum or spray arm by hand when powered off—it should move without grinding. Grinding confirms mechanical obstruction; free rotation with repeated F03 on light loads suggests hall sensor, motor, or inverter board failure requiring professional diagnosis.
prevention_tips: Avoid washing single heavy rugs or shoes that bind the drum; distribute weight evenly to reduce motor strain that precedes F03.
